templates.text: The merchant's eyes glint with borrowed wisdom — every card a debt unpaid.
Knowledge has its price. The merchant knows yours.
Scrolls and tomes line the walls, each one a bargain struck in silence.
They say the merchant once taught kings. Now they trade in fragments of what was.
You leave lighter in gold, heavier in questions you didn't know you had.
A faint smell of old ink and something older. The merchant has been here a long time.
Flashcards spread across the counter like a map of what you don't yet know.
Gold spent on preparation is gold that comes back as victory. The merchant counts on you believing this.
The cards here hold what someone else once memorized in desperation. That desperation transferred with the purchase.
